---
title: "Before You Arrive"
subtitle: "Reading and software to prepare before the workshop"
---

::: {.callout-note}
These materials help participants from both communities build enough background to engage productively from Day 1. You do not need to be an expert in the other field — the goal is familiarity with key concepts and terminology.
:::

---

## Pre-Course Reading

### For Participants from Survival Analysis

These readings introduce infectious disease modeling concepts used throughout the workshop.

**Key concepts to be familiar with:**

- The SIR/SEIRD compartmental model framework
- The basic reproduction number R~0~ and effective reproduction number R~eff~(t)
- Reporting delays and underreporting in epidemic surveillance data
- The difference between individual-level and aggregate (population-level) data

| Authors | Title | Journal | |
|---------|-------|---------|--|
| Grassly & Fraser | Mathematical models of infectious disease transmission | *Nature Reviews Microbiology* (2008) | [Download](assets/literature/Infectious%20diseases/Grassly_Nat2008.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"} |
| Abrams et al. | Hoe mens en wiskunde hand in hand het coronatijdperk kleuren | *NAW* (2020) | [Download](assets/literature/Infectious%20diseases/naw5-2020-21-2-084.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"} |

---

### For Participants from Infectious Disease Modeling

These readings introduce survival analysis concepts and methods used throughout the workshop.

**Key concepts to be familiar with:**

- The hazard function and its relationship to survival probability
- Right-censoring and interval-censoring: what they mean and how they are handled
- Competing risks: what they are and why ignoring them can bias estimates
- Multi-state models: modeling transitions between multiple states (e.g., S → E → I → R)
- Frailty models: incorporating unobserved heterogeneity

| Authors | Title | Journal | |
|---------|-------|---------|--|
| Putter, Fiocco & Geskus | Tutorial in biostatistics: competing risks and multi-state models | *Statistics in Medicine* (2006) | [Download](assets/literature/Survival%20analysis/SiM%20-%202006%20-%20Putter%20-%20Tutorial%20in%20biostatistics%20competing%20risks%20and%20multi%E2%80%90state%20models.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"} |
| Kragh Andersen et al. | Analysis of time-to-event for observational studies: guidance to the use of intensity models | *Statistics in Medicine* (2020) | [Download](assets/literature/Survival%20analysis/Statistics%20in%20Medicine%20-%202020%20-%20Kragh%20Andersen%20-%20Analysis%20of%20time%E2%80%90to%E2%80%90event%20for%20observational%20studies%20Guidance%20to%20the%20use.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"} |
| Pohar Perme, de Wreede & Manevski | What is relative survival and what is its role in haematology? | *Best Practice & Research Clinical Haematology* (2023) | [Download](assets/literature/Survival%20analysis/PoharPerme_relsurv2023.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"} |

---

### Cross-disciplinary Reading

*Papers will be added here*

---

## Software

The workshop uses **R** as the primary computing environment. Please install the following before arriving.

### R and RStudio

- [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) (≥ 4.3.0 recommended)
- [RStudio Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)

### R Packages

#### Survival Analysis

```r
install.packages(c(
  "survival",    # core survival analysis
  "survminer",   # visualization
  "mstate",      # multi-state models
  "flexsurv",    # flexible parametric survival models
  "frailtyEM",   # frailty models
  "timereg",     # additive hazard models
  "cmprsk"       # competing risks
))
```

#### Infectious Disease Modeling

```r
install.packages(c(
  "EpiEstim",    # reproduction number estimation
  "EpiNow2",     # nowcasting and R estimation with delays
  "deSolve",     # ODE solvers for compartmental models
  "odin",        # fast ODE model specification
  "socialmixr"   # contact matrices for structured populations
))
```

#### General

```r
install.packages(c(
  "tidyverse",
  "ggplot2",
  "patchwork",
  "knitr",
  "rmarkdown"
))
```

Verify your installation:

```r
library(survival)
library(mstate)
library(EpiEstim)
library(deSolve)
cat("All core packages loaded successfully.\n")
```

---

## Case Study Data

The case study dataset will be provided to participants ahead of the workshop and posted on the [Case Study](case-studies.qmd) page for download.

::: {.callout-warning}
Please do not share the case study data externally — the complete underlying dataset is revealed at the end of the workshop as part of the learning experience.
:::
